cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cees: noun. plural of cee, the name of the letter C.
cel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Celtic languages.
celery: noun. A European herb (Apium graveolens) of the carrot family.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
clyster: noun. A medicine applied via the rectum; an enema or suppository.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
crest: noun. The summit of a hill or mountain ridge.
cresyl: noun. Any of three isomeric univalent radical derived from a cresol; tolyl
cry: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cori.
cyst: noun. A pouch or sac without opening, usually membranous and containing morbid matter, which develops in one of the natural cavities or in
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eely: adjective. Resembling an eel: long, thin and slippery.
elect: noun. One chosen or set apart.
elects: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of elect
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
erect: adjective. Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.
erects: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of erect
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
ester: noun. A compound most often formed by the condensation of an alcohol and an acid, with elimination of water, which contains the functional
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyer: noun. One who eyes someone or something.
eyre: noun. A journey taken by certain Medieval English itinerant judges (justices in eyre)
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
leer: verb. To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.
leery: adjective. Cautious, suspicious, wary, hesitant, or nervous about something; having reservations or concerns.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
leet: noun. A portion or list, especially a list of candidates for an office; also the candidates themselves.
lest: conjunction. For fear that; that not; in order to prevent something from happening; in case.
let: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lesing-Gelimi.
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).

lyse: verb. To burst or cut a cell or cell structure; to induce lysis.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
reest: verb. Alternative form of rest (“to cure, smoke, or dry (meat or fish); (of a horse) to stop or refuse to go, balk”).
relet: verb. To let a property again
rely: verb. To trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
resect: verb. To remove (some part of an organ or structure) by surgical means.
reset: verb. To set back to the initial state.
rest: noun. Relief from work or activity by sleeping; sleep.
restyle: verb. To refashion (something) in a new style or shape, to fit another purpose.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rete: noun. A network of blood vessels or nerves.
rets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ret
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
scree: noun. Loose stony debris on a slope.
scry: verb. To magically or supernaturally look into or (as an entertainer) predict (the future), using crystal balls or other objects.
sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
secret: noun. A piece of knowledge that is hidden and intended to be kept hidden.
sect: noun. An offshoot of a larger religion or denomination.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seely: adjective. Alternative form of sely or silly.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
set: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sentani.
sleet: noun. Pellets of ice made of mostly- frozen raindrops or refrozen melted snowflakes.
sleety: adjective. Covered in sleet; full of sleet.

slyer: adjective. comparative form of sly: more sly
steel: noun. An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.
steely: adjective. Having qualities resembling those of steel, especially hardness and resoluteness.
steer: verb. To guide the course of a vessel, vehicle, aircraft etc. (by means of a device such as a rudder, paddle, or steering wheel).
stele: noun. An upright (or formerly upright) slab containing engraved or painted decorations or inscriptions; a stela.
stere: noun. A measure of volume used e.g. for cut wood, equal to one cubic metre.
streel: noun. A disreputable woman, a slut.
sty: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siberian Tatar.
stye: noun. A bacterial infection in the eyelash or eyelid.
style: noun. Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.
styler: noun. Someone who styles (especially hair), a stylist.
syce: noun. Alternative spelling of sais (“groom or chauffeur”).
sycee: noun. Any of various gold or silver ingots used as currency in imperial China.

tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
tele: noun. Alternative spelling of telly (“television, telegraph, telephone, teleport, telecommunication”).
terce: noun. The third hour of daylight (about 9 am).
terse: adjective. Of speech or style: brief, concise, to the point.
tersely: adverb. In a brief, concise, or to the point manner.

trey: noun. A playing card or die with the rank of three.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
tyee: noun. A tribal chief of the Chinookan peoples in the Pacific Northwest of the United States.
tyer: noun. Dated form of tier (“a person or device that ties”).

yes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yeskwa.
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
